Building an Internal Developer Platform Is Easy. Getting Developers to Use It Is the Hard Part.

4 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Platform Engineering has become one of the fastest-growing disciplines in modern software delivery.
Analyst firms predict that most large engineering organizations will have dedicated platform teams within the next few years. Companies are investing heavily in Internal Developer Platforms (IDPs), developer portals, self-service infrastructure, and standardized deployment workflows.
The vision is compelling:

• Developers ship software without opening infrastructure tickets.

• Security is built into every deployment.

• Infrastructure becomes self-service.

• Kubernetes complexity disappears behind simple interfaces.
But there’s a problem many organizations don’t talk about.
Building a platform doesn’t guarantee adoption.
Some of the most sophisticated internal platforms end up being bypassed by the very developers they were built to help.
The technology isn’t usually the problem.
